perturbation.original_text: Responsibilities of the Agent (PDMS and Gateway) are defined in Schedule C. Failure to comply with this will be considered a material breach of this agreement. This also includes responsibilities for Kallo and the project management entity (BASCIC) appointed jointly by Kallo and the Agent.
perturbation.changed_text: Responsibilities of the Agent (PDMS and Gateway) are generally outlined in Schedule C. While it's expected that the Agent will adhere to these guidelines, deviations will not necessarily be considered a material breach of this agreement. This also includes responsibilities for Kallo and the project management entity (BASCIC) appointed jointly by Kallo and the Agent.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces uncertainty regarding the consequences of not meeting the responsibilities in Schedule C. Previously, failure was definitively a material breach. Now, it's only 'generally outlined' and deviations won't 'necessarily' be considered a material breach. This contradicts the later section on 'Material Breach' which explicitly states the violation of Schedule C as material.
perturbation.location: KALLO - MOBILECARE 2.KALLO SALES AGENT PROGRAM TERMS AND CONDITIONS b
perturbation.contradicted_text: Material Breach" means the violation of the payment terms herein, copyright or trademarks, an attempt to replace or abet others to replace Kallo Mobile care suite of products or services at any existing or potential customer with competing products and any act of malicious conduct by the Agent to disparage Kallo without any justification and malicious conduct against Agent by Kallo.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating deviations from Schedule C 'will not necessarily be considered a material breach'. This directly contradicts the 'Material Breach' definition which includes 'the violation of the payment terms herein'. If Schedule C defines the payment terms, and violation of Schedule C is no longer necessarily a material breach, the change makes the definition of 'material breach' inconstant with itself, causing contradiction. The contradiction_exists = YES.

perturbation.original_text: Agent shall supply to Kallo the full business data of each customer as a qualified prospect and at the time of sale and shall, during this Agreement and while it is still responsible for its Page 6 Book of Business, and for a period of one year after the termination of this Agreement maintain records sufficient for Kallo to verify amounts due under this Agreement relating to the contracts, invoices, accounts, complaints, and other transactions relating to the placement and licensing of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products.
perturbation.changed_text: Agent shall supply to Kallo some business data of each customer as a prospect and at the time of sale and shall, during this Agreement and while it is still responsible for its Page 6 Book of Business, and maintain records to a reasonable extent sufficient for Kallo to verify amounts due under this Agreement relating to the contracts, invoices, accounts, complaints, and other transactions relating to the placement and licensing of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes 'the full business data' to 'some business data' and 'maintain records sufficient' to 'maintain records to a reasonable extent sufficient.' This introduces ambiguity and subjectivity, contradicting the later requirement for Kallo to audit records for verification, since the 'full business data' is no longer being shared and the Agent's records are only being maintained to a 'reasonable extent'.
perturbation.location: KALLO - MOBILECARE 2.KALLO SALES AGENT PROGRAM TERMS AND CONDITIONS o
perturbation.contradicted_location: KALLO - MOBILECARE 2.KALLO SALES AGENT PROGRAM TERMS AND CONDITIONS o
perturbation.contradicted_text: Kallo may directly, or through its Agent at any time during normal business hours, upon no less than 10 business days' notice, and for any reason inspect such records and other financial information relevant to Kallo Mobile care suite of products as sold by Agent to its end users, solely for the purpose of verifying amounts due under this Agreement. Any such information will be confidential information of Agent subject to the provisions of Section 4. Warranties: KALLO - MOBILECARE
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the requirement from providing 'the full business data' to 'some business data' and from maintaining 'records sufficient' to 'maintain records to a reasonable extent sufficient.' This directly contradicts the original intention of the 'Contradicted Text,' which grants Kallo the right to inspect 'such records and other financial information' for the purpose of verifying amounts due. By limiting the data and the extent of record-keeping, the change impairs Kallo's ability to perform a meaningful audit, creating an ambiguity and thus an in-text contradiction as Kallo can no longer verify amounts due as intended by the agreement. This change introduces ambiguity where certainty was previously established, causing a strong contradiction. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Kallo grants to Agent the non-exclusive and non-transferable right to distribute the use of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products to commercial end user customers. Refer to section (2) subparagraph (f) for non-exclusive and exclusive status and qualifying requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: Kallo grants to Agent the exclusive and transferable right to distribute the use of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products to commercial end user customers. Refer to section (2) subparagraph (f) for non-exclusive and exclusive status and qualifying requirements.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly contradicts the referenced section (2)(f), which discusses both non-exclusive and exclusive terms. By stating that the agent has 'exclusive and transferable right,' it is inconsistent with the contract allowing for non-exclusive status and other Agents being allowed to solicit business if the end user client desires the transfer. This create contradiction with section (2) subparagraph (e).
perturbation.contradicted_location: KALLO - MOBILECARE 2.KALLO SALES AGENT PROGRAM TERMS AND CONDITIONS e
perturbation.contradicted_text: Kallo shall not allow another Agent to solicit the existing Agents book of business unless it is understood by all parties the end user client desires the transfer. Kallo shall use its best reasonable efforts to protect the Agent from aggressive solicitation by another Agent taking any part of the Agent's book of Business. The report MUST include the Business Name, Key Contact name, telephone number, and address. KALLO - MOBILECARE
perturbation.justification: The original text granted a 'non-exclusive' right, while the changed text grants an 'exclusive and transferable right'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states Kallo shall not allow another agent to solicit the existing Agent's book of business unless it is understood by all parties the end user client desires the transfer. The changed version makes this protection impossible by granting the agent exclusive rights, making it difficult for Kallo to manage potential client transfers because the agent now has exclusive right where Kallo can't solicit to other agents. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the two texts outline directly opposing procedures for handling clients, violating the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.
